whatItMeasures: Alkaline phosphatase is an enzyme present in the liver, bones, kidneys, and intestines; elevated levels can indicate liver disease, bile duct obstruction, or bone disorders, and GGT is often ordered alongside it to help identify whether an elevated ALP originates from the liver or bone.

referenceRanges.population: Children and adolescents (growing bone raises ALP)
referenceRanges.source: Mayo Clinic Laboratories — note: ranges vary significantly by age

causesOfHigh: Bile duct obstruction (gallstones, cholangitis, primary biliary cholangitis)
Bone disorders with increased bone turnover: Paget's disease of bone, bone metastases, healing fractures
Vitamin D deficiency (can elevate ALP through increased bone turnover)
Pregnancy (placenta produces ALP, especially in the third trimester — this is normal)
causesOfLow: Hypophosphatasia (rare inherited condition causing very low ALP)

high: Jaundice and itchy skin (in bile duct obstruction)
low: Bone pain and frequent fractures (in hypophosphatasia)
Tooth loss at a young age
